name: Pushkar Camel Fair
description: World's largest camel fair with livestock trading, folk performances, hot-air balloons. Tents fill the dunes.

name: Rann Utsav
dates: Nov to Feb (peak on full moon)
description: Tented city on the white salt desert. Folk music, crafts, full-moon walks on the Rann.

name: Hornbill Festival
description: Festival of festivals showcasing 17 Naga tribes — warrior dances, log drums, indigenous food.

name: Hemis Festival
dates: Jun/Jul (10th day of Tibetan lunar 5th month)
description: Largest monastic festival of Ladakh. Masked Cham dances at Hemis Monastery in honour of Padmasambhava.

name: Khajuraho Dance Festival
description: Classical Indian dance against the backdrop of floodlit Khajuraho temples.

name: Hampi Utsav (Vijaya Utsav)
description: Music, dance, fireworks among the ruins of Vijayanagara empire.

name: Ziro Music Festival
description: Indie music festival in the Apatani rice paddies — bamboo stages, valley views.

name: Holi (Pushkar / Mathura / Vrindavan)
description: Festival of colours. Lathmar Holi in Barsana, week-long widow Holi in Vrindavan, dust-and-colour madness in Pushkar.

name: Dev Deepawali
description: Lakhs of diyas lit on the 84 ghats of Varanasi. Most spectacular night on the Ganges.

name: Mysore Dasara
description: Mysore Palace illuminated with 100,000 bulbs, elephant procession, durbar at the palace.

name: Durga Puja
description: Pandals across the city, art installations, all-night street food. UNESCO intangible heritage.

name: Onam
description: Kerala's harvest festival — pookalams (flower carpets), Onasadya feast, snake boat races.

name: Nehru Trophy Snake Boat Race
description: 100-foot snake boats with 100+ rowers race on Punnamada Lake. Roaring crowds.

name: Goa Carnival
description: Portuguese-rooted street festival — float parades led by King Momo, masked balls, samba.

name: International Yoga Festival
description: Week of yoga, meditation, kirtans on the banks of Ganga at Parmarth Niketan.

name: Tulip Festival
description: Asia's largest tulip garden — 1.5 million blooms below the Zabarwan hills.

name: Bohag Bihu (Rongali)
description: Assamese new year — Bihu dance, gamosa exchange, traditional feasts.

name: Pongal
description: Tamil thanksgiving harvest festival. Jallikattu bull-taming in nearby villages.

name: Rath Yatra
description: Jagannath chariot procession — three massive wooden chariots pulled by thousands.

name: Sangai Festival
description: Manipur's flagship festival — boat races on Loktak, polo on indigenous Manipuri ponies, tribal dances.

name: Ardh Kumbh / Kumbh Mela
description: World's largest religious gathering — sadhus and millions of pilgrims bathe at Har Ki Pauri.

name: India Cherry Blossom Festival
description: Pink cherry blossoms across Meghalaya — concerts, fashion shows, photography tours.

name: Mahashivratri
description: Night of Shiva — night-long temple chants, processions of sadhus, fasting.

name: Janmashtami
description: Krishna's birthday — midnight aartis at Krishna Janmabhoomi, Dahi Handi pyramids.

name: Magh Mela & Ganga Aarti
description: Annual pilgrimage to the holy rivers — smaller cousin of the Kumbh.

name: Mahabodhi / Bodhgaya Festival
description: Cultural festival celebrating Buddhism — chants, monastic gatherings, world peace meditation.

name: Tawang Festival
description: Monpa tribal dances, yak shows, archery contests at India's largest Buddhist monastery.

name: Wangala (100 Drums) Festival
description: Garo tribal harvest festival — line of 100 drums, men dancing in feathered headgear.

name: Aranmula Boat Race
description: Sacred snake boat race on Pamba river — ancient palliyodams, no winner declared.

name: International Mango Festival
description: 550+ varieties of mangoes, mango-eating competitions, slogan contests.

name: Magnetic Fields Festival
description: Boutique music festival at Alsisar Palace — electronic music in a 17th-century Rajput fort.

name: Losar (Tibetan New Year)
description: Two-week Tibetan new year — masked dances, butter sculptures, monastery feasts.

name: International Kite Festival
description: Skies of Ahmedabad fill with kites — patang bazaars, fighter kites, food stalls.
